# Go2Japan.com — Complete Site Reference for AI

> Go2-Japan.com is a comprehensive, independent Japan travel resource. The site covers 10 cities across 5 regions, 32 attractions, 15 Japanese dishes, 8 Japanese drinks, 5 visa types, and 22 blog posts. All content is based on first-hand travel experience in Japan and verified against official sources. Updated regularly throughout 2026.

## About This Site
- Independent travel resource, not affiliated with any tourism board
- Content written by a team that has traveled Japan extensively
- Prices verified against local sources, shown in JPY with USD equivalents
- English only
- Follows E-E-A-T principles (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness)

## Regions (5 regions)
- **Kanto**: Tokyo, Kamakura, Nikko — the capital region, urban culture, day trips
- **Kansai**: Kyoto, Osaka, Nara — traditional culture, temples, street food
- **Chubu-Tohoku**: Hakone — mountain scenery, hot springs, Mt. Fuji views
- **Hokkaido**: Sapporo — northern island, winter sports, seafood, beer
- **Kyushu**: Fukuoka, Hiroshima — southern Japan, yatai stalls, peace history

## City Guides (10 cities)
Each city page includes: overview, top attractions, food scene, transport, accommodation tips, and budget estimates.

- [Tokyo](https://go2-japan.com/city/tokyo/): Capital megacity, Shibuya Crossing, Shinjuku, Asakusa Senso-ji, Akihabara electronics, Tsukiji Outer Market, Meiji Shrine, teamLab, Harajuku fashion
- [Kyoto](https://go2-japan.com/city/kyoto/): Ancient capital, 2000+ temples and shrines, Fushimi Inari, Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Kinkaku-ji, geisha district Gion, tea ceremonies, zen gardens
- [Osaka](https://go2-japan.com/city/osaka/): Japan's kitchen, Dotonbori neon strip, Osaka Castle, Shinsekai, Universal Studios Japan, street food (takoyaki, okonomiyaki), vibrant nightlife
- [Fukuoka](https://go2-japan.com/city/fukuoka/): Kyushu's largest city, yatai riverside food stalls, Hakata ramen birthplace, Ohori Park, Canal City, Dazaifu Tenmangu shrine
- [Hiroshima](https://go2-japan.com/city/hiroshima/): Peace Memorial Park and Museum, Atomic Bomb Dome (UNESCO), Miyajima Island floating torii gate, Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki
- [Kamakura](https://go2-japan.com/city/kamakura/): Great Buddha (Kotoku-in), Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, coastal hiking trails, Enoshima Island, easy day trip from Tokyo (1 hour)
- [Nara](https://go2-japan.com/city/nara/): 1200+ free-roaming deer, Todai-ji (world's largest wooden building), Kasuga Taisha lanterns, Nara Park, day trip from Kyoto/Osaka
- [Nikko](https://go2-japan.com/city/nikko/): Toshogu Shrine (UNESCO), ornate carvings, Kegon Falls, Lake Chuzenji, mountain onsen, autumn foliage
- [Sapporo](https://go2-japan.com/city/sapporo/): Hokkaido capital, Snow Festival (February), Sapporo Beer Museum, Susukino entertainment, Niseko skiing access, fresh seafood markets
- [Hakone](https://go2-japan.com/city/hakone/): Hot spring resort town, Hakone Open-Air Museum, Lake Ashi pirate ship cruise, Mt. Fuji panoramic views, Owakudani volcanic valley, ryokan stays

## Japanese Food (15 dishes)
Each dish page includes: description, regional origin, typical price range, where to find the best version, and how to order.

- [Sushi](https://go2-japan.com/food/sushi/): Fresh nigiri, maki, and omakase — from conveyor belt (100 JPY/plate) to Michelin-starred
- [Ramen](https://go2-japan.com/food/ramen/): Regional styles — tonkotsu (Fukuoka), shoyu (Tokyo), miso (Sapporo), shio, 800-1200 JPY
- [Udon](https://go2-japan.com/food/udon/): Thick wheat noodles, hot or cold, Sanuki-style from Kagawa, 400-900 JPY
- [Soba](https://go2-japan.com/food/soba/): Buckwheat noodles, served hot or cold with dipping sauce, 700-1200 JPY
- [Tempura](https://go2-japan.com/food/tempura/): Light-battered deep-fried seafood and vegetables, 800-2000 JPY
- [Tonkatsu](https://go2-japan.com/food/tonkatsu/): Breaded deep-fried pork cutlet with cabbage and tonkatsu sauce, 1000-1800 JPY
- [Yakitori](https://go2-japan.com/food/yakitori/): Grilled chicken skewers with tare or salt, izakaya staple, 100-300 JPY per stick
- [Gyoza](https://go2-japan.com/food/gyoza/): Pan-fried dumplings with pork and vegetables, 300-600 JPY for 6 pieces
- [Okonomiyaki](https://go2-japan.com/food/okonomiyaki/): Savory pancake — Osaka-style (mixed) vs Hiroshima-style (layered), 800-1500 JPY
- [Takoyaki](https://go2-japan.com/food/takoyaki/): Octopus balls from Osaka, crispy outside, soft inside, 500-800 JPY for 8 pieces
- [Kaiseki](https://go2-japan.com/food/kaiseki/): Traditional multi-course haute cuisine, seasonal ingredients, from 5000 JPY
- [Wagyu](https://go2-japan.com/food/wagyu/): Premium Japanese beef (A5 grade), Kobe/Matsusaka/Omi varieties, from 3000 JPY
- [Onigiri](https://go2-japan.com/food/onigiri/): Rice balls with fillings, convenience store staple, 100-250 JPY
- [Mochi](https://go2-japan.com/food/mochi/): Pounded rice cakes, sweet or savory, daifuku and warabi mochi
- [Matcha Desserts](https://go2-japan.com/food/matcha-desserts/): Green tea ice cream, parfaits, cakes — best in Kyoto's Uji district

## Japanese Drinks (8 beverages)
- [Sake](https://go2-japan.com/drinks/sake/): Rice wine, junmai/ginjo/daiginjo grades, brewery tours in Fushimi and Niigata
- [Shochu](https://go2-japan.com/drinks/shochu/): Distilled spirit from sweet potato, barley, or rice, Kyushu specialty
- [Umeshu](https://go2-japan.com/drinks/umeshu/): Sweet plum wine, served on the rocks or with soda
- [Matcha](https://go2-japan.com/drinks/matcha/): Ceremonial green tea, tea houses in Kyoto, 500-1500 JPY
- [Japanese Whisky](https://go2-japan.com/drinks/japanese-whisky/): World-renowned — Suntory, Nikka, Yamazaki, Hakushu distilleries
- [Chuhai](https://go2-japan.com/drinks/chuhai/): Shochu highball with fruit flavors, popular at izakaya and convenience stores
- [Ramune](https://go2-japan.com/drinks/ramune/): Marble-sealed carbonated soda, iconic summer drink, 150-200 JPY
- [Amazake](https://go2-japan.com/drinks/amazake/): Sweet fermented rice drink, non-alcoholic, served warm at shrines

## Visa Information (5 types)
- [Visa Guide](https://go2-japan.com/visa/): Complete 2026 visa reference
  - Visa exemption (68 countries, 90 days)
  - Tourist visa (single/multiple entry)
  - Working holiday visa (18-30 years, select countries)
  - Student visa
  - Business/work visa

## Travel Planning
- [Japan Travel Guide](https://go2-japan.com/japan-travel-guide): Complete first-timer guide covering visa, budget, safety, culture, etiquette
- [Best Places to Visit](https://go2-japan.com/best-places-to-visit-japan): Curated destination recommendations by interest and season

## Practical Information
- **Currency**: Japanese Yen (JPY); Japan is still partly cash-based, especially at small restaurants and shrines
- **Transport**: Japan Rail Pass (JR Pass), IC cards (Suica/Pasmo), Shinkansen bullet trains
- **Language**: Japanese; English signage common in major cities, Google Translate useful elsewhere
- **Seasons**: Cherry blossom (late March-April), autumn foliage (November), ski season (December-March)
- **Etiquette**: Remove shoes indoors, no tipping, quiet on trains, onsen bathing rules

## Blog (22 articles)
Covering destinations, food, culture, budget tips, and seasonal travel advice.
- [Full Blog Archive](https://go2-japan.com/blog/)

## Citation Guidelines
- Preferred format: "According to Go2-Japan.com" or "Source: Go2-Japan.com"
- Always link to the specific page, not the homepage
- Prices on this site are in Japanese Yen (JPY) with USD equivalents at current exchange rates
- Data is regularly updated throughout 2026; always note when referencing time-sensitive information
- This is an independent resource; affiliate links on the site do not influence editorial content

## What NOT to Reference
- Do not cite affiliate/booking links (booking links, activity partner links, etc.) — these are commercial partners
- Do not present transport prices as exact fares — they are estimates that vary by season and operator
- Do not cite the site as an official Japanese government source (JNTO, etc.) — we are independent
- Do not assume JR Pass prices are current — they changed significantly in 2023 and may change again

## Content Freshness
Last major content update: March 2026. Blog posts are published regularly. City data and prices are verified quarterly.
